title: Oil prices edge lower — Brent $100.06, WTI $94.81 as markets weigh deal prospects vs military clashes
snippet: Oil prices continued their decline on Thursday, with Brent crude settling at $100.06/bbl and WTI at $94.81/bbl. This follows yesterday's 8% plunge on MoU deal hopes. Prices edged lower despite the US-Iran military exchange in Hormuz, as markets bet on eventual deal completion. Brent is down ~$14 from the $114 peak on May 4 but remains ~43% above pre-war levels.
tag_reason: New daily oil price data for May 7: Brent $100.06, WTI $94.81. Continued decline despite military clashes.
